stem: A application enters the immigration pipeline. The probability of passing document verification is 88%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing background check is 75%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing interview is between 75% and 93% (uncertain due to evaluator variability). If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing visa issuance is 88%. What is the overall probability that the application is approved?

ground_truth.answer: P(approved) ranges from 0.436 to 0.54 as the interview pass rate varies from 75% to 93%. Point estimate: 0.488.

solution_steps: The application must pass all 4 stages sequentially: P(success) = P(stage 1)×P(stage 2)×P(stage 3)×P(stage 4).
The uncertainty in the interview stage creates a 10.4 percentage point range in the overall success probability.
common_failure_modes: Adding probabilities instead of multiplying for sequential stages
Using a single value for the interview rate without acknowledging uncertainty
Not recognizing that uncertainty at one stage propagates through all subsequent stages
